frameworks.name: cerebello-thalamo-prefrontal network
frameworks.one_line: interactions between cerebellum and prefrontal cortex support motor skill acquisition

frameworks.name: non-invasive brain stimulation (HD-tDCS)
frameworks.one_line: high-definition tDCS used as causal probe of prefrontal contribution to learning

frameworks.name: explicit motor sequence learning
frameworks.one_line: paradigm in which subjects deliberately acquire a finger-movement sequence

core_question: Does modulating the dorsolateral prefrontal cortex with HD-tDCS alter explicit motor sequence learning?
key_assumptions: skill acquisition is partly supported by cerebello-thalamo-prefrontal interactions
HD-tDCS can transiently alter cortical function under the targeted electrode
anodal and cathodal montages exert dissociable polarity-specific effects
manipulations: HD-tDCS polarity over DLPFC (anodal vs cathodal vs sham)
mixed within- and between-subjects assignment of stimulation
stimulation delivered prior to an explicit motor sequence task
key_findings: anodal DLPFC stimulation impeded motor sequence learning relative to sham
cathodal DLPFC stimulation also impeded motor sequence learning relative to sham
both active polarities produced a negative effect on skill acquisition
interpretations: active prefrontal stimulation exerts an overall negative influence on acquisition of a sequential finger-movement pattern
DLPFC engagement is functionally relevant to explicit sequence learning within the cerebello-prefrontal network
